notes.anchor: M. CICERO greets L. CVLLEOLVS the proconsul
notes.note: The letter-heading; addressed to L. Culleolus, proconsul (probably of Illyricum) about 59 BC. This is the original request on behalf of L. Lucceius's business interests; the companion 13.41 thanks Culleolus for having acted on it.

notes.anchor: the most grateful man in the world
notes.note: L. Lucceius (praetor 67 BC, the historian whom Cicero asked to write up his consulship in Fam. 5.12). Here Cicero commends his commercial interests in Culleolus's province, a dispute with the town of Bullis.

notes.anchor: the men of Bullis have indicated that they will give Lucceius satisfaction at Pompey's arbitration
notes.note: Bullis, a town in southern Illyria, had agreed to settle Lucceius's claim by arbitration before Pompey (Pompei arbitratu) --- a private arbitration in which a powerful patron, here Pompey, served as the agreed judge. Cicero asks Culleolus to back this settlement with his official authority.

notes.anchor: your authority, and your power as governor
notes.note: Auctoritas and imperium: the personal weight and prestige of a Roman magistrate, and the formal power of military and civil command vested in a governor. Cicero asks that both be thrown behind Lucceius's case.
